key_findings: Trace metadata crisis: 10/19 traces had unknown type/source/target fields — this was a critical infrastructure issue
GRAO experience pool was critically small (3 vs 5+ target) — pattern clustering impossible
Early policy saturation warning: 4 consecutive reinforcement rounds without exploration
Gradient failure rate was dangerously high at 52.6% — system was unstable in early phase
All high-priority gradients were metadata-related — infrastructure fix was the bottleneck

resolution_timeline.trace_metadata_fix: Applied in subsequent rounds — metadata fields standardized
resolution_timeline.experience_pool_growth: Grew from 3 → 6 (round 42) — pattern clustering now possible
resolution_timeline.policy_saturation: Still active concern — round 42 shows 6/6 reinforcement proposals, no exploration detected
resolution_timeline.cron_reliability: Under new policies (free/local models, tiered timeouts) — 107+ cycles operational

current_state_bridge.progress_summary: GRAO evolved from early instability (r13) to stable operation (r42). Key fixes: trace metadata standardization, experience pool expansion, policy refinement. Current concern: reinforcement-only mode (6/6 proposals in r42) — no exploration detected, potential saturation risk.

actionable_insights: Round 13's trace metadata crisis was resolved — current system has standardized metadata across all trace types (research, agent, deployment, stability, external-api)
Experience pool growth (3→6) enabled pattern clustering — but could expand further for better signal
Policy saturation concern from r13 (4 consecutive reinforcement) is still active in r42 (6 consecutive reinforcement) — need exploration proposals
5-agent Telegram infrastructure (deployed post-r13) dramatically improved reliability — gradient failure rate went from 52.6% to 0%
GRAO pipeline health: 42 rounds, ~93% plateau, all gradients success — system is stable but potentially stagnant
